name: Ellylldan
description: Ellylldan are elusive and mischievous creatures of Welsh folklore, often associated with the realm between worlds, where magic and illusion intertwine. They are not inherently malevolent, but their actions can often lead to confusion, disorientation, and even misfortune for humans who cross their path. Unlike the more straightforward Tylwyth Teg (fair folk), Ellylldan are more akin to trickster spirits, thriving on ambiguity and playing with perception. They are masters of disguise and shapeshifting, capable of appearing as anything from flickering lights to familiar animals, luring travelers astray or leading them on wild goose chases. Their intentions are often unclear, sometimes offering help in exchange for favors, other times simply causing mischief for their own amusement. Encounters with Ellylldan serve as a reminder of the unpredictable nature of the Otherworld and the importance of caution when venturing into the wild, especially at twilight or in places considered liminal spaces. Ellylldan are said to inhabit ancient forests, hidden glens, and lonely moors, where the veil between the mortal and supernatural realms is thin. Their presence is often marked by strange occurrences, sudden shifts in weather, or the unsettling feeling of being watched. They are a reminder of the ancient magic that still lingers in the Welsh landscape.
subtitle: Mischievous spirits of the twilight realm
appearance: Ellylldan rarely have a fixed appearance, preferring to shift their form to suit their purposes. They may appear as shimmering lights, small animals like foxes or rabbits, or even as distorted reflections of humans. When they do assume a more stable form, they are often described as being small and slender, with pale skin, dark eyes, and mischievous smiles. Their clothing, if any, is usually made of natural materials like leaves, moss, or flowers, blending seamlessly with their surroundings. They often have an unsettling aura, a subtle sense of wrongness that betrays their true nature.
habitat: Ancient forests, hidden glens, lonely moors, twilight zones, liminal spaces
behavior: Ellylldan are primarily mischievous and playful, delighting in confusing and disorienting humans. They may lead travelers astray, hide objects, or create illusions to trick people. While not inherently evil, their actions can have negative consequences, and they are known to make bargains that are difficult to fulfill. They are drawn to places of power and are often found near ancient ruins or sacred sites. They are also said to be sensitive to human emotions, particularly fear and anxiety, which they may exploit for their own amusement.

keyMyths.title: The Lost Traveler
keyMyths.description: A traveler becomes hopelessly lost in the woods after being led astray by an Ellylldan disguised as a friendly guide. The traveler eventually finds their way home, but only after promising a valuable possession to the mischievous spirit.

sources.author: Wirt Sikes
sources.work: British Goblins: Welsh Folk-lore, Fairy Mythology, Legends and Traditions
sources.description: A comprehensive exploration of Welsh folklore, including descriptions of various supernatural creatures.
sources.text: A comprehensive exploration of Welsh folklore, including descriptions of various supernatural creatures.
